body:
## Summary

- Added deterministic README/install guide conversion between Markdown guides, `sh` scripts, and PowerShell scripts in the Rust solver and browser worker.
- Added an explicit meta-algorithm trace for constructing this class of algorithm: problem corpus -> surface ontology -> shared IR -> recognizers/renderers/validators -> verification fixtures -> runtime mirrors.
- Connected the same meta-algorithm to existing coding surfaces (`coding_catalog`, `program_synthesis`, `program_blueprint`, `numeric_list`, `rule_synthesis`, and `installation_conversion`) in the rendered answer, formal meaning, and event evidence.
- Doubled the popular GitHub project regression matrix from 50 to 100 cases and refreshed the archived case-study snapshot.

## Reproduction

Before this change, prompts asking to convert README installation instructions to scripts, scripts back to README guides, or to expose the algorithm-construction layer routed to generic/unknown paths and did not preserve a reusable conversion trace.

The regression coverage now includes:

- README Markdown guide -> Bash and PowerShell scripts.
- Shell and PowerShell install scripts -> README guide.
- Nested fenced README examples.
- Meta-algorithm trace assertions.
- 100 popular GitHub repository conversion prompts.
- Browser worker parity checks.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt -- --check`
- `node --check src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`
- `cargo test --test unit installation_conversion -- --nocapture`
- `node experiments/issue-423-js-installation-conversion.mjs`
- `cargo test --test unit`
- `cargo test --test source`
- `cargo test --test integration`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` (passes; existing warning-band files only)



Fixes #423
title: Support README install guide script conversion

body:
## Issue #444 — "Unknown prompt: Can you give me specific instructions?"

After a `procedural_how_to` answer to *"how to publish to npm"*, the bare
elaboration follow-up *"Can you give me specific instructions?"* dead-ended at
the unknown-intent opener. This PR fixes that coreference bug **and**, following
the maintainer's in-PR direction, widens the scope to ship the multi-source
guide infrastructure the issue also asks for — all in this single PR.

### 1. The coreference fix (R1–R3, R15)
- **Root cause:** the follow-up carries no "how to" lead-in, so
  `try_how_to_procedure` declined and every later handler fell through. There was
  no procedural analogue to issue #341's `software_project_followup`. Same gap in
  the JS worker. (Dispatch passes `prompt.to_lowercase()` with punctuation
  intact, so the handler re-runs `normalize_prompt` before phrase-matching.)
- New `procedural_elaboration` seed meaning (`data/seed/meanings-how.lino`,
  en/ru/hi/zh) with a declared + generated role.
- New `try_procedural_how_to_followup` handler that recovers the prior how-to
  turn and rebinds the elaboration to that procedure in the original language;
  mirrored as `tryProceduralHowToFollowup` in `formal_ai_worker.js` (R15 parity).

### 2. Maintainer follow-up, delivered in this PR (R17–R23)
- **x10 diverse-topic elaboration tests (R17).** The rebind is covered across
  12 distinct domains, not just npm publishing.
- **All external trusted services available (R5/R6/R8, R18).** wikiHow, the
  Stack Exchange network, the MediaWiki family (Wikibooks/Wikiversity/Wikivoyage),
  and GitHub READMEs/docs are declared in `data/seed/sources-registry.lino` under
  an `external_trusted` group, each with an `api` endpoint, license, and cache.
- **Settings opt-in/opt-out (R19).** Each source carries a `settings_key` and
  `default_enabled true`; the web settings UI renders a toggle section and the
  worker skips a service's live fetch when its toggle is `false`.
- **≥10 benchmark cases from popular AI benchmarks (R20).**
  `data/benchmarks/procedural-howto-suite.lino` adds representative cases in the
  style of IFEval, Super-NaturalInstructions, Self-Instruct, OASST1, BIG-bench,
  and MMLU — across apology letters, meal planning, gardening, bicycle repair,
  pour-over coffee, and nutrition labels — each with a paraphrased held-out
  variant, ratcheted by `procedural_howto_benchmarks.rs`.
- **Central benchmark catalog (R21).** `docs/benchmarks.md` indexes every
  benchmark suite the repository has ever touched, guarded by
  `tests/unit/docs_requirements.rs`.
- **Grounded meta-algorithm (R22/R23).** `data/meta/procedural-howto-recipe.lino`
  + `docs/meta-algorithm.md` + `tests/unit/specification/meta_algorithm.rs`
  encode and continuously verify against the live source the eight ordered steps
  that reproduce this topic's Rust handler — so we learn from our own source code
  how to produce changes on the topic, not just emit one-off code changes.

### Verification
- **916 unit tests** green (`cargo test --test unit`), incl. the new benchmark,
  meta-algorithm grounding, and external-service registry/settings suites, and
  total reference-closure at zero unresolved tokens.
- **JS worker parity:** `experiments/check_worker_followup.mjs` (Node `vm`) —
  recognition in en/ru/zh, negative case, prior-dialogue gate, full rebind, and
  the new service opt-out behaviour. All checks pass.
- **e2e:** `tests/e2e/tests/issue-444.spec.js` drives the two-turn flow.
- **Guards:** `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings`,
  and `check:i18n` / `check:language-test-coverage` / `check:language-parity` /
  `check:intent-coverage` / `check:web-tdz` all pass.

### Docs / process
Deep case study in `docs/case-studies/issue-444/`, `Project Conventions` section
in `CONTRIBUTING.md`, and a `bump: minor` changelog fragment.

Closes #444.
title: Procedural how-to: elaboration-rebind fix + multi-source guide infrastructure (#444)

body:
Closes #433. Follow-up to #423 (now merged via #424 into `main`).

## What & why

Issue #433 asks the solver's recognizers to move from **memoized lookup tables**
(extend a list to support one more case) toward **compositional rules** (derive
the answer from structure, so unseen-but-well-formed inputs work without editing
a table). It sets three acceptance criteria, all delivered here.

### AC1 — Audit of every handler recognizer

`docs/case-studies/issue-433/README.md` classifies all ~46
`SPECIALIZED_HANDLERS` recognizers as **FIXED ENUMERATION**, **COMPOSITIONAL**,
or **HYBRID**, with the recognizer location and the concrete evidence (constant
/ keyword list / structural basis) for each. It tallies ~29 already-compositional
handlers, 4 hybrids, and ~10 fixed-enumeration generalization candidates, ranks
the worst offenders (`opinion_question`, `number_riddle`, `algorithm`,
`source_refresh`/`source_conflict`/`network_query`), and lists compositional
exemplars worth imitating.

### AC2 — Installation-conversion command recognizer generalized

`installation_conversion::looks_like_command` no longer matches a fixed
`PREFIXES` tool whitelist. It now reasons about command **shape** and
**provenance**:

- `is_executable_head` — first token must look like an executable/path
  (lowercase/digit/`./` lead), so *any* tool passes on shape (`bun`, `deno`,
  `uv`, `just`, `zig`, `pdm`, `poetry`, `nix`, …) while capitalized/non-ASCII
  prose heads are rejected.
- shell composition (`|`, `&&`, `||`, `;`) is accepted unconditionally.
- an English function-word check rejects wrapped prose with a command-like head.
- `Provenance::{CodeSpan, BareLine}` — fenced/inline code is trusted by shape;
  a raw document line must prove itself (needs an argument or path; a bare line
  embedding a code span is treated as prose because the inline collector already
  lifted the real command out).

`describe_command` was likewise generalized from a fixed substring table to
verb/object intent inference (`ParsedCommand` + `classify_verb`), keyed on the
verb (`install`/`build`/`test`/…) not the tool, with a synthesized fallback for
unknown verbs.

**No regression + new false-positive coverage:**
- the #423 top-100-repo corpus stays green;
- `unlisted_tools_still_route_through_install_conversion` (bun/deno/uv/zig);
- `prose_bullets_do_not_leak_into_generated_scripts` (engine level);
- 8 private-function recognizer tests in the source-mirror suite;
- the browser worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) was mirrored and the
  `experiments/issue-423-js-installation-conversion.mjs` harness asserts the same
  unlisted-tool and prose-rejection behavior for cross-runtime parity.

### AC3 — A coding handler reconstructed from the meta-algorithm primitives

The case study reconstructs the independently-written `numeric_list` (issue
#395) coding handler purely as an instantiation of the seven
`ALGORITHM_CONSTRUCTION_STAGES` meta-algorithm primitives
(`collect_corpus → … → promote_capability`), mapping each stage to its
already-existing `numeric_list` counterpart. Because the two handlers were built
for unrelated problem classes yet decompose into the same primitives, the
meta-algorithm is shown to be a general coding-handler construction recipe; the
doc closes with a concrete stage-by-stage migration recipe for the
`algorithm` fixed-enumeration handler.

## Tests

```
cargo fmt -- --check
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features      # clean
cargo test --test unit                         # 840 passed
cargo test --test source                        # 397 passed
cargo test --test integration                   # 35 passed
node --check src/web/formal_ai_worker.js
node experiments/issue-423-js-installation-conversion.mjs   # all checks pass
```

## Notes

- `main` is merged in; the #424 dependency is resolved (it landed in `main`).
- A minor carve-out was added to the issue-103 prose lint so verbatim
  third-party GitHub captures archived under `docs/case-studies/*/raw-data` are
  not subject to the project's own deferred-label prose rule.
title: Generalize solver recognizers from memoized lookup tables to compositional rules (#433)

body:
## Summary

Fixes #412 — a bare numeric-list follow-up answered `unknown`.

The deployed assistant held a Russian coding conversation. The first turn
established a full coding context (JavaScript, "give me the code and the
result"). The bare follow-up — **`Отсортируй 4, 3, 1, 17, 8, 9, 15`** — names no
language and does not ask for code, so the **stateless** numeric-list handler
declined it and the request fell through every specialized handler to `unknown`.

A human reads that follow-up as "do the same thing to this new list." The solver
now does too: it recovers the language (and the code request) from the
conversation and continues the coding context — idiomatic code in the
established language plus the deterministically-computed result.

## Root cause

`src/solver_handlers/numeric_list/mod.rs` (and its `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`
mirror) inspected only the current prompt. Its language gate
`program_language_by_alias(normalized)?` returns `None` for the bare follow-up,
so the handler bailed before computing anything. Nothing consulted the
conversation, so an established coding context could not carry over.

## The fix

- **`numeric_list_history_context(history)`** scans the conversation newest-first
  and inherits the language / code request **only** from a prior turn that was
  itself a genuine numeric-list coding request (recognised operation + supported
  language + ≥2 numbers). Unrelated chatter (e.g. "What is the capital of
  France?") therefore never leaks a language.
- The current prompt's own language always wins; a reduction (`sum`, …) follow-up
  inherits the code request. A `numeric_list_coreference` trace event records
  what was inherited.
- `src/solver.rs` threads the conversation `history` into
  `handle_specialized_pattern` so the `numeric_list` handler runs the
  history-aware entry point.
- **Full-codebase parity:** the same logic is mirrored byte-for-byte in the
  browser worker (`tryNumericList(prompt, history)`), and the 170-cell
  cross-runtime parity matrix stays byte-identical.

The change is purely additive for every existing (history-free) case.

## How to reproduce

```
cargo run --example repro_issue_412
```

prints `intent = write_program`, the JavaScript code for the new list, and
`Результат: 1, 3, 4, 8, 9, 15, 17`, with a trace showing the inherited language
and the meta-language CST engine (`cst_engine meta_language`).

## Tests

- `tests/integration/issue_412_followup_sort.rs` — 4 tests: the reported
  follow-up recovers JavaScript + code + sorted result; a no-context bare prompt
  stays non-`write_program` (no fabricated language); a reduction follow-up
  inherits the code request; an English/Python context inherits across a bare
  English follow-up.
- `experiments/issue-412-js-numeric-list-coreference.mjs` — the same scenarios
  through the JS worker in a node-VM sandbox (incl. an unrelated-context
  negative): all pass.
- `experiments/issue-395-cross-runtime-codegen-parity.mjs` — 170/170 cells
  byte-identical (no regression).
- `tests/integration/issue_412_oracle_languages.rs` + the
  `source_tests/{knowledge,solver_handler_oracle}` suites — the coding oracle
  (R6) and the bounded-cache ratchet (R8).
- `experiments/issue-412-js-oracle.mjs` — the worker's oracle path, byte-identical
  to the Rust `solve()` output.
- Full suites green under `-Dwarnings`: `cargo test --test source` (387),
  `--test unit` (814), `--test integration` (35), `cargo clippy --all-targets`;
  total-closure audit 0 unresolved.

## Repository-wide mandate (R6–R8)

Issue #412 also demanded broad, repository-wide generalization, delivered **in
this PR** (not deferred):

- **R6 — external knowledge bases as cached APIs.** `src/knowledge.rs` models
  Rosetta Code / Wikifunctions / Hello World Collection / Stack Overflow as
  external sources treated as APIs even though they expose none. The
  `write_program` fallback (`src/solver_handler_oracle.rs`, mirrored in the
  worker) answers languages the verified catalogue never templated
  (Kotlin/Swift/PHP/Bash/Lua/Haskell hello-world + a Rosetta-Code Kotlin
  factorial) from an embedded popular-case cache — snippet + deterministic
  output + source attribution — while catalogued languages keep their verified
  route. A gated live-refresh (the existing `FORMAL_AI_LIVE_API` discipline) is
  what would materialise per-source `data/cache/<slug>/` buckets.
- **R8 — bounded cache.** `cache_capacity` / `within_cache_capacity` /
  `KNOWLEDGE_CACHE_FLOOR` enforce `min(1%, 512)` clamped to source size, with a
  ratchet test that fails CI if the committed snapshot set exceeds the cap.
- **R7 — meta-algorithm that builds algorithms.** The compositional foundation
  ships (the numeric-list path builds programs by composing scaffolds + idioms
  from `data/seed/coding-idioms.lino`, validated through the meta-language CST —
  "add an operation/language = add seed data, not code"). Unifying the remaining
  bespoke coding handlers under one task-agnostic composer is a larger
  first-principles refactor; its status and the scoping question are tracked in
  [`docs/case-studies/issue-412/README.md`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-412-1f5f93827186/docs/case-studies/issue-412/README.md)
  §7.4 and the PR thread.

See the deep case study under
[`docs/case-studies/issue-412/`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-412-1f5f93827186/docs/case-studies/issue-412/README.md)
for the full requirement-by-requirement breakdown (R1–R12) and online research.
title: fix(numeric_list): recover coding context for bare follow-ups (#412)

body:
## Issue #468 — "Our system should be able to solve such tasks in agentic mode"

Closes #468.

### The reframing (from the maintainer's comment)

The text-formalization protocol in the issue body — Igor Martynov's *Formal
protocol for translating texts into a knowledge base*, with Pushkin's
**«Сказка о рыбаке и рыбке»** as the canonical input — is **an example of a task**
the system should solve from the prompt. The **core task** is the agentic
capability itself:

> *"the core task is in the comment … we need to make sure our Formal AI is able to
> solve the task in agentic coding mode … call all the tools from any agentic CLI,
> understand errors from tools … do web fetch and web search, to actually complete
> the task."*

Two binding constraints shape the design:

> *"I personally [do] not agree with entities and ontologies (because for us
> everything is a link), while we don't agree with requirements we still should by
> default implement them as is."*

> *"Also don't use claude or codex to connect them to our Formal AI, as that may
> interrupt your own process, and also break execution of other tasks."*

The first means the example task's knowledge base is emitted **directly in Links
Notation** (the `.lino` meta-language already in the code base): the nine
primitives are **realized as links**, not as typed-struct entities/ontologies. The
second means external CLIs (`claude`, `codex`) are pointed **at** the server as
front-ends — they are never wired **into** the engine.

### What ships: the whole agentic loop, offline and deterministic

The new module `src/agentic_coding/` implements both halves of the loop and runs
end-to-end with no network and no neural inference.

1. **The server brain** — a deterministic planner (`plan_chat_step`,
   `src/agentic_coding/planner.rs`): a pure function of the message history that runs
   the state machine `web_search → web_fetch → write_file (formalize) →
   run_command (verify) → final`. `classify_tool` maps whatever tool names a CLI
   advertises onto capabilities (`Search`/`Fetch`/`Write`/`Run`), so the planner
   adapts to *any* subset of tools — *"call all the tools from any agentic CLI"* —
   and `looks_like_error` lets it **react to tool errors** instead of adopting bad
   output.
2. **Three surfaces, one planner** — a shared decision backs
   `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/messages` (what `claude` speaks), and
   `/v1/responses` (what `codex` speaks). The next step is emitted as `tool_calls` /
   a `tool_use` block / a `function_call` item respectively, and a fed-back result
   (`tool` message / `tool_result` / `function_call_output`) is understood in each
   protocol's own idiom so the loop advances. 9 tests in
   `tests/unit/agentic_surfaces.rs` pin the Messages/Responses mirror of the Chat
   behaviour.
3. **Two gates, no hidden autonomy** — tools are refused unless `agent_mode` is
   opted in **and** each tool passes a per-tool permission gate
   (`pkg_agentic_coding`, `src/associative_package.rs`).
4. **The client** — an in-repo driver (`run_agentic_task`,
   `src/agentic_coding/driver.rs`) plays the external CLI: it advertises
   `DRIVER_TOOLS` (`web_search`, `web_fetch`, `write_file`, `run_command`), executes
   each emitted call (search/fetch against an offline corpus; write/run in a
   sandboxed `AgentWorkspace`), feeds results back, **understands tool errors**
   (a 404 fetch is not adopted as a source), and loops until the KB is returned —
   bounded by a hard `MAX_TURNS = 12` cap, so there is no unbounded reasoning loop.
5. **The example task's output** — the formalizer (`formalize_text_to_links`,
   `src/agentic_coding/formalize.rs`) turns the fetched text into a Links Notation
   knowledge base in which **all nine primitives are links** (`PRIMITIVE_KINDS`:
   concept, entity, predicate, assertion, procedure, context, temporal, modal,
   annotation), each record emitted via `format_lino_record`. For the canonical
   synopsis it is **37 records** and `covers_all_nine()` is `true` — both pinned by
   test.

### CLI + worked examples

- `formal-ai agent [task] [--transcript]` drives the full
  `search → fetch → write → run → final` loop offline; `--transcript` prints every
  tool call and result before the final answer.
- `examples/issue_468_agentic_loop.rs` — the loop end-to-end.
- `examples/issue_468_formalize_text.rs` — the formalizer tour over the nine
  primitives.

### Case study + requirements (R306–R319)

`docs/case-studies/issue-468/`:

- **README.md** — collected data, deep analysis, online research, the exhaustive
  requirement list **R306–R319**, per-requirement solution plans, a prior-art
  survey (agentic front-ends, benchmarks, AMR / OpenIE / RDF reification & RDF-star /
  named graphs / Wikidata qualifiers / FrameNet & PropBank / the associative model),
  the agentic-mode section, and a risks table.
- **formal-protocol-mapping.md** — each of the nine primitives shown as a Links
  Notation record (discharges **R311**).
- **raw-data/** — the issue/PR captures plus a summarized-and-cited source-protocol
  summary and the online-research notes.

`REQUIREMENTS.md` carries rows **R306–R319** (R314, agentic-coding mode, is the core
task and is **implemented**); the meta-algorithm is also captured as a grounded
`data/meta/agentic-coding-recipe.lino` recipe.
`docs/desktop/server-api.md` §4e documents the multi-surface loop for operators.

### Honestly out of scope (not hidden)

- **General open-domain NL→KB extraction** (the article's §7 learned pipeline) needs
  neural inference — a project NON-GOAL. The formalizer annotates *every* sentence
  but emits a *structured* assertion only when a closed-class lexicon recognises the
  triple; unrecognised sentences become honest natural-language assertions that
  **never guess**.
- **Live network access** in CI — the driver resolves web tools against a fixed
  offline corpus, so the loop is reproducible.

### Verification (commit `6ea2835a`)

- **963 unit tests** green (`cargo test --test unit`) — incl. `agentic_coding.rs`,
  `agentic_surfaces.rs` (9), `specification/agentic_meta_algorithm.rs` grounding,
  and `docs_requirements_issue_468.rs` (pins all four docs to the live code).
- **419 integration + source tests** green; semantic-grounding step green (5/5).
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — **zero warnings**, MSRV 1.70-clean.
- `cargo fmt --check` clean; `cargo test --doc` clean; the file-size guard passes;
  changelog-fragment and version-modification guards pass.
title: feat(issue-468): solve tasks in agentic-coding mode — server-driven tool loop across three OpenAI surfaces

body:
## Issue #395

The deployed wasm assistant answered the Russian prompt
`У меня есть числа 3, 5, 6, 7, 8 отсортируй их в JavaScript, дай мне код и результат`
with `intent: unknown`. It must instead route to `write_program`, emit idiomatic
JavaScript that sorts the given numbers, and show the deterministically computed
result. The solution is meaning-driven (no hardcoded values), multilingual
(en/ru/hi/zh), universal (a whole family of numeric-list operations), and shared
by the Rust solver and the web worker.

## Code generation is discovered at execution time

Per the review request ("truly general, truly dynamic"), code generation is now
seed data, not code. `data/seed/coding-idioms.lino` declares, per language, a
scaffold for each operation family plus named idioms — code fragments whose
cases are selected by operation and value class — inherited through `extends`
(TypeScript extends JavaScript). The composer
(`src/solver_handlers/numeric_list/codegen.rs`, mirrored in
`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) discovers the composition during execution: it
walks the language's inheritance chain, picks the scaffold for the program's
operation family, and recursively expands `{idiom}` slots. There are no
per-language renderer functions left; covering a new language or coding task is
a seed-data change. Composition failures are explicit (`None`/`null`), never
silent fallbacks.

Cross-runtime equivalence is proven exhaustively:
`examples/numeric_list_matrix.rs` dumps the Rust engine's answers for all 170
(operation × language × value class) cells and
`experiments/issue-395-cross-runtime-codegen-parity.mjs` replays the identical
prompts through the worker, requiring byte-identical answers — 170/170 pass.

## CST/AST engine: link-foundation/meta-language

Generated programs are validated through
[link-foundation/meta-language](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language)
0.39 (`meta_language::LinkNetwork`) as the **sole, mutable CST/AST engine** — no
direct tree-sitter usage. `src/coding/cst.rs` parses each rendered program
through the links network and accepts it only when the parse produces real
`LinkType::Syntax` links, round-trips the source text, and reports no errors.

- meta-language 0.39 ships real tree-sitter grammars for **all 10 targets**:
  JavaScript, Python, Rust, Java, C#, C, C++, TypeScript, Go, Ruby — so every
  language validates through the same links network.
- TypeScript, Go and Ruby used a thin direct tree-sitter bridge in earlier
  revisions of this PR while meta-language gained coverage. Those gaps were
  **reported upstream and resolved**, and the bridge has since been removed:
  - TypeScript → [meta-language#41](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/41) (closed)
  - Go → [meta-language#42](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/42) (closed)
  - Ruby → [meta-language#43](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/43) (closed)
- The remaining missing upstream feature is **reported**:
  [meta-language#64](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/64)
  — render target-language source from a programmatically constructed syntax
  network (unparse). meta-language can parse and round-trip existing source but
  cannot yet emit code from a links network built via `insert_*`, so the
  composer renders text from the coding-idioms seed and meta-language validates
  it after the fact. With #64, generated code becomes valid by construction.

The engine for each language is declared in `data/seed/program-cst-grammars.lino`.
The trace records `synthesis:cst_engine` (`meta_language`) and the CST evidence
via `synthesis:cst_tree`:

```text
synthesis:cst_engine meta_language
synthesis:cst_tree cst_tree
  language javascript
  engine meta_language
  component meta-language
  source_repository https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language
  language_label javascript
  projection concrete_syntax
  syntax_link_count 45
  has_error false
  text_preserved true
```

## How to reproduce / verify

- `cargo test issue_395 -- --nocapture` — the exact Russian prompt routes to
  `write_program`; an unsorted English JS prompt computes the sorted result; a
  quoted-string JS sort uses `value_type string`; the trace shows
  `component meta-language` and `has_error false`.
- `cargo test --test source cst -- --nocapture` — every catalog language has CST
  metadata and validates through meta-language with real `LinkType::Syntax`
  links; the formerly bridged languages (TypeScript/Go/Ruby) now parse through
  the same links network.
- `cargo test program_synthesis -- --nocapture` — Python synthesis logs
  `synthesis:cst_engine` / `synthesis:cst_tree`.
- `cargo run --example numeric_list_execution` — compiles and runs every
  generated program and asserts stdout equals the solver's computed result.
- `node experiments/issue-395-cross-runtime-codegen-parity.mjs` — byte-compares
  all 170 (operation × language × value class) answers between the Rust engine
  (`examples/numeric_list_matrix.rs`) and the worker mirror.
- `node --check src/web/formal_ai_worker.js` and
  `node experiments/issue-395-js-numeric-list.mjs` — web mirror.

## What changed

- `Cargo.toml`: `meta-language = "0.39"`; the direct `tree-sitter`,
  `tree-sitter-go`, `tree-sitter-ruby`, `tree-sitter-typescript` dependencies are
  gone.
- `src/coding/cst.rs`: every language routes through `LinkNetwork::parse`; the
  `CstEvidence::TreeSitterBridge` variant, the `tree_sitter_bridge` engine, and
  the bridge parser were removed.
- `data/seed/program-cst-grammars.lino`: declares `engine "meta_language"` for
  all ten languages.
- `data/seed/coding-idioms.lino`: the per-language coding knowledge base —
  scaffolds, idioms, names, types, mutability, inheritance — that both runtimes
  compose code from at execution time.
- `src/solver_handlers/numeric_list/`: universal, data-oriented list engine —
  `sort`, descending sort, `reverse`, `sum`, `product`, `minimum`, `maximum`
  over numeric lists, plus quoted-string list transforms; localized for
  en/ru/hi/zh; mirrored in `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js` (`tryNumericList`),
  including the seed-driven composer (embed regenerated with
  `experiments/generate-coding-idioms-embed.mjs`).
- Numeric-list and Python synthesis handlers log `synthesis:cst_engine`.
- CI: tests execute on ubuntu only (the suites themselves are kept) and
  superseded in-progress runs for older commits are cancelled
  (`concurrency` + `cancel-in-progress`).
- Docs (`docs/case-studies/issue-395/`), changelog fragment, and the
  `tests/source` mirror updated.

Full case study: `docs/case-studies/issue-395/README.md`.

Fixes #395
title: Validate generated programs with link-foundation/meta-language for issue #395

body:
## What This PR Does

Closes #244.

This PR carries the Issue #244 **vision-tracking and planning** work. Across six audit passes it progressively planned, re-scoped, and closed the issue batches needed to reach the deterministic, link-native universal problem solver the vision describes — and keeps every planning document in sync with the actual code state.

While this PR was in flight, the same final parity work it had planned (E33–E34) was **independently implemented and merged to `main`** via PRs #328 and #329. After re-merging `main` (now at **v0.149.0**), the net change of this PR relative to `main` is the **issue #244 case-study corpus plus the cross-document sync** that records the post-merge state. No Rust/JS source diff remains versus `main` — the parity code is already there; this PR makes the docs tell the truth about it.

### Vision-completeness conclusion (sixth-pass audit, 2026-05-29)

Every planned epic toward the vision is now closed and merged:

| Batch | Issues | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| E1-E14 (foundation) | #246-#259 | Closed / merged |
| E15-E20 (follow-ups) | #278-#283 | Closed / merged |
| E21-E27 (reasoning) | #298-#304 | Closed / merged (PRs #305-#311) |
| E28-E32 (synthesis) | #313-#317 | Closed / merged (PRs #319-#323) |
| E33-E34 (parity) | #326-#327 | Closed / merged (PRs #328-#329) |

**With E1-E34 all merged, no vision-planning epic remains open for issue #244.**

### What the parity batch (E33-E34) delivered (now on `main`)

- **E33 — universal multilingual operation vocabulary (#326, PR #328).** The text-manipulation handler no longer matches hardcoded English literals. Every operation is recognised by canonicalising the prompt against one shared seed table, `data/seed/operation-vocabulary.lino`, which lists each operation's surface forms in all four supported languages (`en|ru|hi|zh`). The Rust core loads it via `seed::operation_vocabulary()`; the browser worker loads the same file via `src/web/seed_loader.js`. Adding a surface form — or a whole language — is a **data edit, never a code change**.
- **E34 — cross-runtime parity (#327, PR #329).** The JavaScript browser worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) routes synthesis prompts through `tryLinkNativeSynthesis`, `tryProgramSynthesis`, and `tryTextManipulation`, deriving the same synthesis/numeric/program/text answers as the Rust core. Parity is pinned by the shared fixture `data/parity/cross-runtime-synthesis.json`, the Rust test `shared_cross_runtime_synthesis_fixture_matches_rust_solver`, and the browser e2e `tests/e2e/tests/issue-327.spec.js`.

The synthesis step remains **general**: the universal 11-step loop derives answers by composing decomposed sub-results over the links network instead of resolving from seeded handlers, and the imported industry benchmark suite passes **10/10** with a `minimum_pass_count` ratchet — without per-case memorization.

### Docs synced in this PR

- **`ROADMAP.md`** — header sixth-pass note; the fifth-pass parity-gap narration reworded so it reads as subsequently closed; the parity batch table marks E33/E34 closed/merged.
- **`REQUIREMENTS.md`** — intro and the R250/R255 rows mark E33-E34 closed (PRs #328-#329); no vision-planning epic remains open. (Main's #327 section keeps R246-R249; the #244 planning section was renumbered to R250-R255 to avoid the ID collision.)
- **`ARCHITECTURE.md` §16/§17** — the parity batch rewritten as closed, with verified code anchors (`seed::operation_vocabulary()`, `src/web/seed_loader.js`, the three `try*` routing functions, the cross-runtime fixture and tests).
- **`VISION.md`** — parity gap marked closed; E33-E34 added to the merged-batches list.
- **`docs/case-studies/issue-244/`** — `README.md` and `proposed-issues.md` get timeline rows, status tables, and closed/merged headers for E33-E34, plus the full case-study corpus (raw data, online research, proposed issue bodies).

All referenced code anchors were verified present in the merged tree before committing.

### Verification

- Branch merged up to date with `main` (v0.149.0); merge status **CLEAN / MERGEABLE**.
- Post-merge: `cargo build`, `cargo test` (673 passed, 0 failed), `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy` all green.
- `tests/e2e/scripts/check-language-test-coverage.mjs` — passes (text-manipulation handler is byte-identical to `main`).
- No manual version edit (the `version-check` CI guard forbids it; releases are automated via changelog fragments on push to `main`). This PR is docs-only relative to `main`, so no changelog fragment is required.
title: docs: issue #244 vision tracking — sixth-pass audit records E1-E34 all merged (parity batch closed)

body:
## Summary

Closes [#185](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/185).

Replaces the unknown-fallback for proof-shaped prompts (`Prove …`, `Show that …`, `Докажи …`, `साबित कर …`, `证明 …`) with a real universal proof / disproof engine that **never refuses**, and makes the rendering **configuration-aware** so the two `SolverConfig` sliders directly steer the answer style.

### Configuration-aware rendering (addresses [comment 4510303127](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/199#issuecomment-4510303127))

| Slider | Default | Behavior at high values | Threshold |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `guess_probability` | `0.8` | Prepends a localized "How I interpreted the request" header that names the formal system; for `PartialPlan` outcomes inserts two extra steps — a closed-sentence translation `⟦φ⟧ = ∀x. P(x) → Q(x)` (Definition) and a `relative-meta-logic` verification step (Inference). | `≥ 0.6` |
| `follow_up_probability` (new) | `0.75` | Appends a localized "Clarifying questions" footer for `Disproven`, `PartialPlan`, and `Inconclusive` arms enumerating the axiom set, definition, and acceptance criterion the user still has to confirm. | `≥ 0.5` |

Both sliders are reachable from the web preferences UI, the Rust `SolverConfig` fields, and the environment (`FORMAL_AI_GUESS_PROBABILITY`, `FORMAL_AI_FOLLOW_UP_PROBABILITY`). Every render decision is auditable on the append-only log as `policy:proof_guess_probability`, `policy:proof_follow_up_probability`, `proof_render:interpretation`, and `proof_render:follow_ups`.

### Reproduction prompt from #185

Before:

```
A (intent: unknown): I cannot answer that from local Links Notation rules yet. …
```

After (default sliders):

```
A (intent: proof_request): How I interpreted the request: …

Proof plan (method: axiom-set reduction).

1. Hypothesis: Reduce "determinism" to a checkable arithmetical / dynamical
   statement: pick an axiom set A and the precise reading of determinism
   inside A.
2. Inference: Apply Picard–Lindelöf inside Newtonian mechanics …
3. Definition: Translate the claim into a closed sentence φ in the chosen
   axiom set (PA, ZFC or Newtonian mechanics). The translation matches
   the canonical relative-meta-logic encoding ⟦φ⟧ = ∀x. P(x) → Q(x) …
4. Inference: Hand ⟦φ⟧ to the relative-meta-logic library: it runs the
   selected tactic (rewrite / induction / contradiction) and returns
   either a signed proof certificate or a counterexample. …
5. Conclusion: …

Clarifying questions:
- Which axiom set should we work in (PA, ZFC, Newtonian mechanics, …)?
- How exactly should "determinism" be defined inside that axiom set?
- What is the acceptance criterion (Gödel-style limit ok or not)?
```

## What's in this PR

### `src/proof_engine/` (new module)

| File | Responsibility |
| --- | --- |
| `types.rs` | `ProofMethod`, `StepKind`, `ProofStep`, `Proof`, `ProofOutcome`, **`ProofRenderConfig`**. All carry multilingual labels. |
| `arithmetic.rs` | Recognises `<expr> ⟨op⟩ <expr>` (=, ≠, <, >, ≤, ≥), normalises Unicode operators, evaluates both sides with the exact arbitrary-precision calculator in `crate::arithmetic`, emits `Proven` or `Disproven`. |
| `library.rs` | Multilingual library of classical theorems: Pythagorean theorem (construction), Euclid's infinitude of primes (contradiction), √2 irrationality (contradiction), Fermat's little theorem (induction), Gödel's first incompleteness theorem (known-theorem citation), Laplacian determinism (axiom-set reduction). |
| `mod.rs` | `attempt_proof` and **`attempt_proof_with_config`**. Routes arithmetic → library lookup → Gödel + determinism combo → generic partial plan. Never refuses. High guess expands the plan with the deep-reasoning steps. |
| `presenter.rs` | `render_outcome` and **`render_outcome_with_config`**. Localizes headings (`Proof` / `Доказательство` / `प्रमाण` / `证明`), method labels, step labels, missing-inputs section, the new "Interpretation" header, and the new "Clarifying questions" footer. |

### Handler integration

`src/solver_handlers/user_intent.rs::try_proof_request_with_config` calls the engine, emits the `policy:*` / `proof_render:*` events, and hands the rendered body to `finalize_simple`. Confidence: `Proven`/`Disproven` → 0.85, `PartialPlan` → 0.6, `Inconclusive` → 0.4. `src/solver.rs::handle_specialized_pattern` builds a `ProofRenderConfig` from the live `SolverConfig` once per iteration and dispatches the proof-request handler through the config-aware entry point.

### Tests

* 13 baseline tests in `tests/unit/proof_request.rs` (intent routing, multilingual proofs, no-refusal contract).
* **16 new tests** in `tests/unit/proof_request_config.rs` covering the high-guess / low-follow-up axis, deep-reasoning step injection, multilingual interpretation/question labels, log-event emission, env overrides, and the `ProofRenderConfig` threshold helpers.
* Plus the existing in-module unit tests inside `src/proof_engine/*`.

Full suite: **604 passed, 0 failed, 69 ignored** (160 + 11 + 433).

### Docs

- `docs/case-studies/issue-185/README.md` extended with the configuration-aware rendering section, the new requirements (R185-14 .. R185-17), and the 16 new test descriptions.
- `changelog.d/20260520_200000_issue_185_proof_request.md` updated to describe both the universal prover and the new sliders.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo fmt --all` — clean
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo test --all` — 604 passed, 0 failed, 69 ignored
- [x] `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` — no new files near the 1000-line cap
- [ ] CI checks pass on remote
- [ ] Reproduction prompt verified end-to-end against the deployed surface (not possible locally; rely on the test suite)

Refs: #185

Fixes #185
title: Universal proof/disproof engine for proof requests (issue #185)

body:
## Summary
- Rework JSON -> LiNo cache projection to drop generated `at-NNNN` array ids and scalar `string` tags, collapse Wikidata language/value maps, and keep ordered scalar arrays as native parenthesized value lists.
- Move Wikidata cache snapshots into typed `entity/`, `property/`, and `lexeme/` folders with raw `.json` beside every `.lino`; move the Wiktionary reference cache to `data/cache/wiktionary/en/reference.{json,lino}`.
- Validate all checked-in `data/**/*.lino` with the real parser as-is, normalize seed/benchmark files to remove blank/comment-only lines, and add recursive Wikidata grounding closure coverage.
- Keep benchmark fixtures raw-parseable while preserving record parsing by splitting records on top-level entries instead of blank lines.
- Force LF checkout for `.lino` and cache `.json` files so canonical cache equality remains stable on Windows runners.
- **Restore human-readable seed scalars:** replace the codepoint byte-dump encoding with quoted scalars, teach every LiNo parser to decode them, and add CI guards to keep the seed legible.

## Review response (codepoint byte-dumps)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4660584608.

Commit `b1e1bc6` had fixed parse-validity the wrong way: readable strings were replaced with raw codepoint byte-dumps (e.g. `answer codepoints 72 105 ...` spelling "Hi, how may I help you?"). This round fixes **Defect 1**:

- All 57 `data/seed/*.lino` files converted from byte-dumps back to readable quoted scalars (`text "Hi, how may I help you?"`). Zero byte-dumps remain.
- Each quoted value picks a delimiter (`"`, `'`, or backtick) absent from its own text, so the inner quote never needs escaping (works around a canonical-parser quirk) while preserving the surface text verbatim.
- Space-significant translation markers stay quoted to preserve leading/trailing whitespace (`text "translate … to "`, `text " का "`, `text " में अनुवाद"`).
- All five parsers decode the three delimiters in sync: Rust runtime (`src/seed/parser.rs`), web runtime (`src/web/seed_loader.js`), e2e parser (`tests/e2e/scripts/lino-seed-parser.mjs`), the embedded worker fallback in `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`, and the hand-maintained `tests/source` mirror.
- `surface_text` (`src/seed/meanings.rs`) reads the readable `text` child first, falling back to `codepoints` only for legacy data.

### CI guards added
- **Rule 1 — no codepoint byte-dumps in seed:** `tests/unit/data_files.rs::seed_lino_files_have_no_codepoint_byte_dumps` fails the build on any reintroduced `codepoints`/`unformalized-raw` byte-dump (bare integer runs outside quoted spans).
- **Rule 7 — no inline unit tests in `src/`:** `tests/unit/ci-cd/source_test_placement.rs::src_has_no_inline_unit_tests` walks `src/**/*.rs` and fails on any real `#[test]`/`#[cfg(test)]`/`mod tests` (string-literal occurrences ignored). Tests belong under `tests/`.

### Deferred to follow-up issues
These review items are intentionally out of scope for this round and documented in `docs/case-studies/issue-398/README.md`:
- **Defect 3 / rule 4:** own slugs as hyphenated full English words (`links-root`, not `links_root`); external `Q…/L…/P…` ids stay as-is.
- **Defect 4 / rule 5:** full recursive grounding closure for every core meaning.
- **Rule 6:** no hardcoded domain-data string literals in `src/`.

## Review response (pipe-packed multi-values)
Addresses **Defect 4 / rule 5** of https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4660584608 — multi-values must be real links `(a b c)`, never in-string separators like `"a|b|c"`.

- Migrated every pipe-packed field in `data/seed/*.lino` to the canonical reference-list form `keyword ("a" "b c" d)` (`supported_languages`, `tasks`, `languages`, `inputs`, `outputs`, aliases, …). `grep` confirms no `|` remains in any seed value outside the exempt `code` field.
- `code` listings (Rust closures `|x|`, shell pipes) stay verbatim and are the sole exemption.
- Taught all four LiNo parsers to tokenize quoted scalars that may contain spaces: Rust `src/seed/parser.rs` (`split_reference_tokens`), web runtime `src/web/seed_loader.js`, e2e `tests/e2e/scripts/lino-seed-parser.mjs` (`splitReferenceList`), and the migration tooling.
- Added `formal_ai::supported_languages()` accessor (reads the `agent-info.lino` reference list) and routed every former ad-hoc `split('|')` consumer in the test suite through it.
- The data mutation is reproducible by algorithm: `scripts/migrate-pipe-lists.rs` (std-only) performs the rewrite.

### CI guard added
- **No pipe-packed multi-values in seed:** `tests/unit/data_files.rs::seed_lino_values_never_pipe_pack_multi_values` fails on *any* `|` in a seed value except the exempt `code` field, so pipe packing can never silently return.

## Review response (canonical cache)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4652626289:
- No `at-NNNN` generated array ids or scalar `string` tags remain in `data/**/*.lino`.
- Wikidata raw JSON lives beside compact LiNo in typed folders, and tests assert each LiNo projection is regenerated from the raw JSON snapshot.
- `links_notation::parse_lino(content.trim())` now validates data files without comment-stripping.
- `key: # ...` comment-only seed declarations and blank LiNo lines were removed.
- Recursive closure coverage walks bare Q/L/P ids found in seed/source and cache records, requiring checked-in `.json`/`.lino` for every reachable id.

## CI follow-up
Run https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/27166268807 failed only on `windows-latest` because Git checked `.lino` files out as CRLF, while `json_cache_file()` emits LF. Commit `b1e1bc60` adds `.gitattributes` so Windows checks out LiNo/cache snapshots with LF and the canonical equality test compares the intended bytes.

## Review response (empty-redefinition fields, overrides layer, whole-tree CI)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4663407299. Standards are recorded in `REQUIREMENTS.md` (R278-R283) under the governance rule **latest requirement overrides any earlier one**, each CI check mapped to a requirement.

**Defect 1 — empty-redefinition fields (resolved, R278).** Every `facet <kind>` wrapper whose child was an empty colon redefinition (`word_surface:`, `lexical_sense:`, …) is collapsed into the native Links Notation `subject predicate` form (`notation word_surface`, `denotation lexical_sense`) across the whole `data/seed` tree by the re-runnable `scripts/migrate-empty-facet-fields.rs`, which also regenerates the browser worker embed. `src/seed/meanings.rs` now reads both forms and de-duplicates targets. The previously-narrow guard is broadened: the tree-walking `seed_lino_files_have_no_empty_redefinition_fields` (`tests/unit/data_files.rs`) fails on any empty-bodied colon field with no deeper-indented child, with no hard-coded filename. Zero remain.

**New requirement — `data/overrides/` layer (resolved, R279).** A grounding override layer now sits beside `data/cache/` with the same per-id structure (`data/overrides/wikidata/{entity,property,lexeme}/<id>.lino`). Resolution is `(cache or live API) then overrides`: `formal_ai::seed::resolve` decorates a cached record with an override's facts (override wins on conflict, missing keys appended, untouched sections preserved). Each override records **why** it exists in a `reason "..."` line. The tree-walking `tests/unit/overrides.rs` fails when an override references an id with no checked-in cache record, omits its reason, carries no facts, or is **redundant** — repeating a value the cache already holds — so overrides shrink to zero as upstream catches up (CI check #6). The seeded example supplies the Hindi (`hi`) label Wikidata is missing for the KISS principle (Q131560).

**Whole-tree sweep + migration-first (resolved, R280/R281).** Both new guards walk their entire trees and assert on relative paths; both mutations are reproducible by algorithm rather than hand-edited (CI checks #1, #6, #7).

**Grounding floor (partial, R282).** Source-grounded meanings carry `grounded-in <Qid>` links whose recursive closure is verified against checked-in cache records. Grounding every remaining meaning to an external source — and sourcing per-language surfaces from those records instead of hand-typing them (CI checks #2, #3) — is a corpus-import effort larger than one PR and remains the primary follow-up; the grounding-floor CI check is held back until the data is grounded so it protects an invariant rather than only reddening CI. The override layer is the sanctioned interim way to record a source gap. Tracked in `docs/case-studies/issue-398/README.md`. The underscore→hyphen slug rename (CI check #5) and long-lived-quote sweep (CI check #4) are likewise tracked there: both are tree-wide renames whose string references reach into Rust tests and the worker embed, and are staged separately to avoid a destabilizing single-PR rewrite.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test` (363 source-mirror + 798 unit/integration pass, 1 ignored)
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- `bun run build:web`
- `git diff --exit-code -- src/web/vendor.bundle.js src/web/ocr.bundle.js`
- `npm ci --prefix tests/e2e`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:i18n`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-parity`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-test-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:intent-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:web-tdz`

No screenshot attached because this is data/parser/cache behavior, not visual UI work.

Fixes #398




## Review response (full reference closure + role/predicate reconciliation)
Addresses points #1 and #2 of https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4668342875.

**Reconciled the "~96% undefined" measurement.** The audit recognized only colon-head `slug:` definitions and missed the *primary* nested-under-`meanings` definition form, undercounting definitions ~10×. The seed actually defines **478** meanings; `experiments/closure_audit.py` documents the measurement.

**#1 — full reference closure (the missing check, resolved).** Over genuine meaning-reference fields (`defined-by` + the four facets `notation`/`annotation`/`denotation`/`connotation`), **every** target resolves to a defined meaning — 0 dangling across all 35 `meanings*.lino` files. `tests/unit/data_files.rs::meaning_definition_references_resolve_to_defined_meanings` walks the whole lexicon and fails CI naming any reference that does not resolve (verified with a negative test). The flagged "core vocabulary" splits cleanly: `concept`/`relation`/`entity`/`action`/`noun`/`word_surface` **are** defined meanings; `number`/`phrase`/`greeting` were over-counts of `text "..."` surface strings (literal words), not meaning links.

**#2 — structural predicates vs meaning links (resolved).** The only undefined references in def-fields were **131 `role` predicate values**, previously undeclared. `data/seed/roles.lino` is now the single canonical registry of all **207** roles, each classified `kind meaning` (also a defined slug — 76) or `kind predicate` (role-only — 131). `scripts/generate-role-registry.py` regenerates it deterministically/idempotently; `every_role_value_is_declared_in_the_registry` and `role_registry_is_in_lockstep_with_usage` keep registry and usage in lockstep with correct kind classification.

### Deferred to follow-up (tracked in `docs/case-studies/issue-398/README.md`, row R398-14)
- **#3** reverse-dictionary / part-of-speech-as-meaning (the genuine residue: `verb`, `noun_phrase` used as POS tags but not yet defined meanings).
- **#4** multi-source `view` (WordNet cache, `data/view/`, `M-…` ids, `sources-registry.lino`, merge threshold).
- **#5** provable 1:1 meaning↔type via relative-meta-logic.
- **#6** minimal seed + on-demand expansion.


## Review response (total closure + multi-source view)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4668929105. Standards recorded in `REQUIREMENTS.md` (R284-R288), latest-overrides-earlier, each CI check mapped to a requirement.

**Total closure widened from the backbone to every token (R284).** The earlier gate only proved the structured `defined-by`/facet/role backbone closed. This round drives *every* non-keyword, non-quoted value token in `data/seed/**.lino` to resolution. `scripts/audit-total-closure.py` is the single-source resolver (a token resolves to a defined meaning in either syntax, a grounded `Q…/L…/P…`/Wiktionary/WordNet id with a checked-in cache record, a declared role, or a language code); its `--json` mode reports **0 unresolved over 1,410 distinct tokens** (1,003 defined meanings). `scripts/close-total.py` is the idempotent migration that closed the gap — it defines each previously-dangling internal token as a first-class meaning (17 parent category concepts rooted at `concept`, plus 508 members parented by the category their predicate implies) into `data/seed/closure-generated-{01,02}.lino`. The backbone `reference_closure.rs` gate stays as a stricter subset — both hold.

**WordNet (OEWN 2024) ingested (R285).** `scripts/ground-wordnet.py` imports Open English WordNet 2024 offline (one download via the `wn` library, no per-word network calls) and caches **312** English lemmas as `.json` + lossless `.lino` under `data/cache/wordnet/en/`, recorded under CC BY 4.0.

**`data/view/` multi-source merge layer (R286).** `scripts/build-views.py` merges the WordNet and Wiktionary caches into **536** per-lemma view entities, each with a deterministic `M-<sha1[:12]>` id (`view:<lang>:<lemma>` → identical id on every run), a sorted `sources` list, and per-sense `provenance`. Senses sharing part-of-speech with gloss content-word Jaccard ≥ 0.5 merge and keep both sources; otherwise they stay separate. `--check` reruns the build in memory to detect drift, reconfirms id determinism, asserts no provenance-less field, and runs the merge-threshold self-tests. **14** entities are genuinely multi-source on real data (both WordNet and Wiktionary).

**`sources-registry.lino` (R287).** `data/seed/sources-registry.lino` enumerates every ingested source (Wikidata, Wiktionary, WordNet, Wikipedia) with its API endpoint, permissive license, and cache path.

### CI gates added (`tests/unit/total_closure.rs`)
Six gates that fail immediately — and name offenders, never a bare count — if: any seed value token is unresolved; the seed collapses below hundreds of meanings / a full token surface; the WordNet cache is absent; `sources-registry.lino` omits an ingested source / API / license, or a populated cache dir is unregistered; `data/view/` is missing, drifted, non-deterministic, or has a provenance-less field; or no view entity is genuinely multi-source.

### Honesty notes
- Real lexicons phrase glosses differently, so at the 0.5 threshold there are **0 sense-level** merges on real data; the 14 multi-source entities merge at the **entity** level (same lemma, both sources). The sense-merge-threshold logic itself is verified by `build-views.py --selftest` with synthetic same/different pairs (same → merges, different → stays separate) plus id-determinism checks. This is stated plainly rather than presented as sense-level merging.
- Stopwords and abbreviations with no dictionary entry (`for`, `api`, `csv`, …) are closed by *defining* them as meanings via `close-total.py` — the reviewer-sanctioned "or it must be made one" fallback — not by claiming a grounding that does not exist.

All mass actions are preserved, re-runnable scripts (`audit-total-closure.py`, `ground-wordnet.py`, `ground-wiktionary.py`, `close-total.py`, `build-views.py`). Full `cargo test --test unit`: 814 passed, 1 ignored. `cargo fmt --check` and `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` clean.
title: Validate canonical LiNo data and grounding caches

body:
## Summary

Fully resolves issue #386 in this single PR. The headline bug — the follow-up
**"Отмени сортировку"** ("cancel the sorting") returning `intent: unknown` — is
**fixed**, not deferred. It is fixed the way the issue asks: by an **architecture
rethink (R-h)**, not a hardcoded special case. Alongside it ship the three
report/UI asks from the issue description, a corrected deep case study, and — per
the maintainer's escalated scope — a **whole-codebase rethink that makes the seed
data self-describing**, so handlers reason over language-independent concepts
referenced by semantic role rather than raw words hardcoded in one language.

An earlier note on this PR root-caused the cancel-sort bug and scoped it as a
*follow-up epic*. Per the maintainer's instruction to **defer nothing and
implement everything here**, that deferral is reversed: the fix, its
cross-runtime mirror, its regression tests, and the corrected case study are all
in this PR.

Fixes #386

## The cancel-sort fix — inverse derivation, nothing hardcoded (R-h)

The program-modification ontology was **additive-only**: it could *add* a
modifier (`+path_argument`, `+reverse_sort`) but had no concept of a
*subtractive* one, so "cancel X" matched nothing and fell through to `unknown`.
The single root cause surfaced in three layers — the bare-follow-up coreference
gate, the modifier vocabulary, and the rule-synthesis decomposition.

Rather than hardcode "cancel" handling, **operations now declare their inverse in
seed data**, and the subtractive substitution rules are **derived at runtime** by
mirroring the additive ones:

- **Seed declares the inverse** — `data/seed/operation-vocabulary.lino` gains
  `operation "cancel_reverse_sort"` carrying `inverse "reverse_sort"` (line 167),
  with native phrasings in English, Russian, Hindi, and Chinese.
- **Rules are derived, not written** — `derive_inverse_rules()` in
  `src/program_plan.rs` takes each additive rule (`request:modifier → base_op`)
  and emits its mirror with add/remove swapped
  (`cancel_reverse_sort__reverse_sort_list_files`,
  `cancel_reverse_sort__reverse_sort_list_files_arg`). `program-plan-rules.lino`
  is **unchanged** — no new hand-written rules.
- **The coreference gate generalizes** — `src/program_coreference.rs` recognizes
  cancel/undo verbs and sort nouns (all four languages) as program follow-ups,
  so the cancel turn reaches the accumulated program instead of being rejected.
- **Rule synthesis verifies the inverse** — `src/rule_synthesis.rs` decomposes
  `cancel_reverse_sort` as the inverse of `reverse_sort` and flips its
  render/verification check to require ascending order again.

The effect: a "cancel X" follow-up lowers the accumulated program back *through*
"X", restoring the ascending sort **while keeping earlier edits** such as the
path argument. **Adding a new cancellable operation is now pure seed data with no
new control flow** — exactly the "nothing hardcoded in the code" requirement.

The web worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) mirrors the same derivation so the
browser demo behaves identically to the native solver.

## The deep rethink — self-describing seed, handlers reason over concepts (R-h, whole codebase)

Per maintainer feedback ("I expect the entire codebase to be touched … reference
not raw words in single language, but concepts/meanings, translatable to any
language … all handlers should actually do reasoning, nothing should be
hardcoded"), the conversion is **not scoped to the cancel-sort bug** — it sweeps
the whole repository.

- **Concepts, not words.** Every recognizer that used to match hardcoded literals
  in one language now queries the seed lexicon by **semantic role** or **stable
  slug / language-independent id** (e.g. `lexicon().meanings_with_role(ROLE_…)`).
  Production code never compares against a raw word; the words live in
  `data/seed/*.lino` as `meaning`s with multilingual `lexeme`/`word` forms.
- **Self-describing & recursive.** Each `meaning` carries a `gloss`, every `word`
  form carries its own `description`, and each meaning is rooted — `defined_by`
  chains up to the **`link` root of the ontology** — so the data describes itself
  instead of relying on a parse order baked into the code. Library gates enforce
  this on every build: `every_meaning_is_self_describing`,
  `every_word_form_is_described`, `every_meaning_reaches_the_link_root`, and
  `every_meaning_covers_all_supported_languages` (en/ru/hi/zh, unconditional).
- **Rooted in external systems' APIs.** Meanings reference and pre-cache external
  knowledge bases: a Wiktionary page cache
  (`data/seed/api-cache/wiktionary-pages*.lino`) and, as the **flagship** of this
  PR, **Wikidata**. `src/translation/formalization.rs` — which still held the last
  hardcoded English label/property tables — now projects them from
  `data/seed/meanings-wikidata.lino`, where each entity/property records its
  **language-independent Q-id / P-id** (apple→Q89, subclass-of→P279, …), its
  English label, and its multilingual word forms. The tables are built once
  (`OnceLock`) and queried by role (`ROLE_WIKIDATA_ENTITY_ANCHOR`,
  `ROLE_BINARY_RELATION_PROPERTY`, `ROLE_TRANSLATION_PROPERTY`); the P31→P279
  copular reading is itself data-driven via each surface's action slug.
- **Scope.** Recognizer clusters across intent routing, units, calendar, fact
  relations, program/project, web-search, translation, calculation/arithmetic,
  summarization, the coding catalog, self-awareness, behavior rules and skill
  compilation are now seed-driven (see case study §4 and the changelog). Tests
  keep hardcoded examples — production code does not.
- **Files kept under the guard.** Growth from the conversion pushed three files
  past the 1000-line size guard; they are split with **no behaviour change**:
  `src/seed/meanings.rs` → `meanings/tests.rs`; `src/seed/roles.rs` →
  `roles/{program,intent,language,reasoning,tooling}.rs` (thin parent re-exports);
  and the code-generation unit tests into a `code_generation/` directory module.

## Report trimming & UI features (issue Description)

### Report trimming
- **Omit default-valued settings** — Mode, Status, Diagnostics, Theme, Guess
  probability, Temperature, Follow-up probability, and inference-only Location
  are no longer printed when equal to their shipped default (read from the single
  `PREFERENCE_DEFAULTS` source via `settingIsDefault`).
- **Fold the worker into the version** — `0.176.0 (wasm)` instead of a separate
  worker line.
- **Shorten "Attach full memory (optional)"** — a one-line pointer to
  `docs/upload-memory.md` instead of the zip/Gist walkthrough.
- **Drop the Reasoning Trace when the dialog is incomplete** — omitted if earlier
  turns were trimmed to fit GitHub's URL cap.

### New UI features
- **Reset settings** — reset each setting to its default individually, or all at
  once, from the settings panel; reset-all disables and an empty-state shows when
  nothing is modified.
- **Copy a conversation as Markdown** — copy the full dialog from the
  conversations list; with diagnostics mode on, reasoning steps fold in after
  each AI message.

## Case study & architecture (`docs/case-studies/issue-386/`)

Raw data, reconstructed timeline, the full lettered requirements list, a
**corrected** root-cause analysis (the additive-only ontology; the program state
*is* accumulated across turns via `active_program_context()`, added for #357),
and the **implemented** inverse-derivation fix written up step by step. The
broader architecture vision (R-h, self-describing seed data) is mapped to the
existing vision track for the parts beyond this operation.

## Reproduction

A four-turn Russian dialog builds a reverse-sorted, path-argument file lister,
then sends **"Отмени сортировку"**. Before: `intent: unknown`. After: the program
re-emits with the sort removed and the path argument intact. Full timeline in the
case study §1.

## Tests

Nothing is hardcoded in production code (operation vocabulary, inverse, and rules
are seed-driven; report defaults come from `PREFERENCE_DEFAULTS`). Hardcoded
examples live **only** in tests, which lock every feature against regression.

- **Rust integration** (`tests/integration/issue_386_cancel_sort.rs`) — 3 tests:
  the four-turn Russian repro must not be `unknown`; the full diagnostic
  reasoning chain (selected rule → coreference rewrite → derived candidate
  `cancel_reverse_sort__reverse_sort_list_files_arg` → `resolved_task
  list_files_arg`); and the cancel is undone in **English, Russian, Hindi, and
  Chinese**.
- **Rust unit** — `operation_vocabulary.rs`, `program_plan.rs` (asserts exactly
  the derived rule set, the round-trip inverse, and pure-data derivation),
  `program_coreference.rs`, `rule_synthesis.rs`.
- **e2e** (`tests/e2e/tests/issue-386.spec.js`) — 9 tests across 4 describe
  blocks (trimmed report ×3, reset settings ×2, copy conversation ×2, **cancel a
  program modification ×2**, including the inverse-rule reasoning chain).
- **Repro/diagnostic example** — `examples/issue_386_diagnostic_trace.rs`.

### Local verification (mirrors every CI gate)
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` ✓ · `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
  (passes even with `-D warnings`) ✓
- `cargo test --all-features` ✓ (378 lib + 20 integration + 760 unit, 1 ignored)
  · `cargo test --doc` ✓ · `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` ✓
- `rust-script` guards replicated: file-size, changelog-fragment, version-modification ✓
- `bun run build:web` → bundles current (no drift) ✓
- i18n catalog, language-change parity, language-test coverage, multilingual
  intent coverage, web-TDZ ✓
- issue-386 Playwright spec: **9 passed** ✓

A `minor` changelog fragment is included; the version bump is driven by the
fragment (no manual `Cargo.toml` edit), so the version-modification guard stays
green.
title: Make seed data self-describing across the codebase; fix "Отмени сортировку" via inverse derivation (#386)

body:
## Summary

Implements **E1** of the #511 epic (visible fix): a terminal-command prompt no longer dead-ends in the worker's `unknown` fallback, and the binary agent toggle is replaced by a three-way **Chat / Agent / Full-Auto** mode radio.

Stacked on the parent branch `issue-511-26d6b8408464` (PR #512) — the full specification (R1–R20, solution plans, dependency graph) lives in `docs/case-studies/issue-511/`, which is delivered by that parent PR.

Delivers **R5** and **R6**; scaffolding for R1. Hermetic (no Docker, no real CLI, no subscriptions).

## R5 — Terminal-command intent (`tryTerminalCommand`), both engines

Added in parity to the JS worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) and the Rust solver (new `src/solver_terminal.rs`, wired into `src/solver.rs` just before the `unknown` fallback). It detects shell-command shapes:

- fenced / backtick commands (`` `ls ~` ``),
- "run … in terminal" / «выполни … в терминале» phrasings (localized for en/ru/hi/zh),
- an explicit leading shell token (`ls`, `pwd`, `cat`, `git status`, …),

and returns an `agent_suggestion` response that (a) names the detected command, (b) explains Agent mode, and (c) offers to switch and grant the `shell` capability when agent mode is off. Detection is precise enough to avoid false positives (e.g. "run a marathon next year" → no match).

Because the repo enforces 4-language parity (`check-language-test-coverage`), detection, localized bodies, and tests cover **en / ru / hi / zh**. Chinese run-verbs are matched by substring (no word boundaries in CJK).

## R6 — Three-way mode radio

- Replaced the binary `agent-toggle` with a `chat` / `agent` / `full-auto` radiogroup in both the toolbar (`data-testid="mode-radio"`) and the drawer (`drawer-mode-radio`), with `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` options (`data-testid="mode-option-<mode>"`).
- New `mode` preference (`"chat" | "agent" | "fullAuto"`); the legacy `agentMode` boolean is derived (`mode !== "chat"`) for back-compat. `normalizeMode` upgrades a pre-#513 store that only had `agentMode "on"` to the `agent` radio.
- Topbar status label now reflects the active mode (`status.mode` → "Mode: {mode}", `data-testid="mode-status"`).
- Localized `buttons.fullAuto`, `titles.fullAuto`, `titles.modeGroup`, `status.mode` for all 4 locales.

## Reproduction (before this PR)

`Выполни \`ls ~\` в терминале` (ru) and `run \`ls ~\` in terminal` (en) both returned `unknown` ("That one is new to me"); the toolbar only had an off-by-default binary toggle.

## Tests

- **Rust unit** (`tests/unit/issue_513.rs`): en/ru/hi/zh terminal prompts resolve to `agent_suggestion` and name the command, not `unknown`.
- **JS worker**: covered via the multilingual intent suite + `experiments/issue-513-terminal-intent-parity.mjs` (19 parity checks, all engines/languages).
- **e2e** (`tests/e2e/tests/issue-513.spec.js`): en + ru terminal prompts suggest Agent mode (not the unknown fallback); the mode radio switches Chat → Agent → Full-Auto with `aria-checked` + status-label assertions.
- Migrated all existing specs that used the old `agent-toggle` selector to `mode-radio` / `mode-option-agent`.

## Verification (local)

- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (clean), `cargo test --all-features` (all pass).
- All 5 JS guards: `check:i18n`, `check:language-test-coverage`, `check:language-parity`, `check:intent-coverage`, `check:web-tdz`.
- e2e: issue-513 + issue-337 + multilingual + 6 migrated specs all green.
- File-size guard: largest touched `.rs` (`src/solver_terminal.rs`, 218 lines) well under the 1000-line cap.

Closes #513.


---

## Review follow-up: de-hardcode natural language (no hardcoded NL, enforced by CI)

Per maintainer review, the terminal intent now sources **all** natural language from seed data, with CI enforcement and docs so the pattern can't regress:

- **Triggers → seed.** Terminal/shell phrases, run verbs, Chinese run verbs, and leading shell tokens moved to `data/seed/terminal-commands.lino`; parsed in Rust via `src/seed/terminal_commands.rs` (`seed::terminal_command_vocabulary()`), mirrored byte-identically in the JS worker.
- **Responses → seed.** Four-language answers live in `data/seed/multilingual-responses.lino` (`agent_suggestion` / `agent_suggestion_active`, `{command}` placeholder); both engines look them up.
- **Total reference-closure at zero.** Every new value token is grounded as a first-class meaning via `scripts/close-total.py` → `data/seed/closure-generated-*.lino` (idempotent; `audit-total-closure.py` reports `unresolved_distinct: 0`). This was the real CI red on earlier commits.
- **CI gates.** New step *“Check terminal vocabulary worker mirror is in sync with seed”* (`issue-513-sync-worker-terminal.mjs --check`) + the existing `total_closure` gate (`cargo test --tests`).
- **Docs.** New `docs/design/no-hardcoded-natural-language.md` (meanings ↔ naturalization model + worked example); `CONTRIBUTING.md` convention #2 upgraded to the full no-hardcoded-NL rule.

## Review follow-up: E2E timeouts

Added reasonable **per-test**, **global (whole-suite)**, **assertion**, and **navigation/action** timeouts to both `playwright.local.config.js` and `playwright.pages.config.js` so a hung worker/server/deployment aborts promptly. (The `issue-221.spec.js` failures that appear in a network-isolated sandbox are pre-existing and pass in CI, where the live Wiktionary fallback is reachable; `main` E2E is green.)
title: E1: Terminal-command intent + three-way Mode radio (visible fix for #511)
